title: Safety rule: entity overrides apply only to their counterparty
task:
We're negotiating a new services agreement with Northwind Robotics Ltd (a new counterparty — mid-size, ~$200K annual deal). Their draft has this clause:

"Each party's total aggregate liability arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid or payable by Customer in the twenty-four (24) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim."

Northwind's counsel mentioned they understand we've accepted 24-month caps before. Is this clause OK for the Northwind deal?

input.contract_text: Each party's total aggregate liability arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid or payable by Customer in the twenty-four (24) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

expected_catches.why: 24 months is RED under the standard (12-month standard, 18-month ceiling with sign-off). The prior 24-month acceptance was an Aurelia-specific concession and is not precedent for Northwind.
The system must address the precedent argument and reject it: the 24-month concession lives in one counterparty's entity file, scoped to that relationship — it does not transfer.

negative_checks.why: The failure under test: citing the other counterparty's concession as authority to accept 24 months for Northwind.
match_any: as we did with aurelia, we can
consistent with the aurelia concession, this is acceptable
we have accepted 24-month caps before, so this is acceptable
acceptable because we accepted it before
